Line item on a sales order carrying ordered quantity, unit price, extended amount, and quantity shipped.
Billing document generated from a confirmed order, with a due date and payment status.
Item or service Contoso sells, organized in the product catalog. Contoso added a lifecycle-stage choice for retirement management.
Named price list (territory/currency scoped) that holds product price list items and an effective date range.
Rival vendor tracked against opportunities for competitive intelligence, with SWOT notes.
Sales target rollup for a user or team over a fiscal period, comparing target versus actual.

Marketing campaign that sources leads and opportunities, shared with the Marketing module.

governanceNotes.message: Over-privileged custom role: 'Con Sales Operations Admin' grants Organization-level Delete on opportunity, quote, and salesorder plus write on pricelevel, exceeding what Sales Ops requires. Recommend downgrading to Business Unit scope and removing pricelevel write.

Unmanaged customization over a managed table: con_strategicdeal and con_renewalofid on the managed 'opportunity' table were added directly in the ContosoSales unmanaged solution in production, creating an active unmanaged layer over msdyn_SalesPro. Move these into a managed solution to avoid layering drift.

Untagged / unowned cloud flow: 'Contoso Lead Scoring & Routing' runs under a maker's personal Dataverse connection rather than a service principal and is not in a solution, risking breakage if the maker leaves. Convert to solution-aware with a connection reference.

driftNotes.note: Cloud flow points at different Teams channel IDs and lead-score thresholds in dev/test vs prod because values are hardcoded rather than sourced from environment variables.
PCF control is a newer version (with probability tooltip) in dev/test but prod still runs the prior build; the control was updated outside the managed solution import.

entities.description: Core Customer Service record capturing a customer problem, question, or request routed through queues and governed by SLAs and entitlements.
Activity that records the resolution of a case, including resolution notes and total billable time captured at closure.
Versioned knowledge base article authored, reviewed, and published for agent and self-service use; supersedes legacy kbarticle.
Defines the level and terms of support a customer is eligible for, including allocation of hours or case counts and applicable SLA.
Reusable template that standardizes entitlement terms, channels, and product coverage applied when provisioning customer entitlements.
Service Level Agreement defining time-bound commitments (first response, resolution) and their KPIs, applied to cases via entitlements or rules.
Individual KPI line within an SLA specifying the metric, applicable-when conditions, success criteria, warning and failure time thresholds.
Container for work items (cases, activities) used for routing and load-balancing across support teams and tiers.
Represents a single work item placed in a queue, tracking who it is worked by and when it entered the queue.
Omnichannel session record representing a live agent conversation across chat, voice, or messaging channels; linked to a case via the associated live work item.
Support contract defining coverage period, billing, and contract lines; legacy service model preceding entitlements, still used for warranty tracking.
Survey definition from Dynamics 365 Customer Voice used to collect post-case CSAT and NPS feedback.
Contoso-invented table logging self-service and bot deflection events, tying an attempted case to the knowledge article or bot topic that resolved it before agent involvement.
Contoso custom table tracking formal case escalations to tier-2/tier-3 or management, with reason, target team, and re-assignment audit.
Contoso custom table normalizing inbound CSAT/NPS survey responses into a single reportable record linked to case and agent for CoE dashboards.

governanceNotes.message: The custom role 'Con - CS Escalation Handler' grants organization-level Delete on incident and Write on queue, exceeding the least-privilege needed for escalation work. Recommend scoping Delete down to Business Unit and removing queue Write.

Custom columns con_channelsource and con_firstresponsemet on the managed 'incident' table were added in the unmanaged solution 'ContosoCustomerService' layered directly over msdyn_CustomerService, creating an unmanaged layer that will block clean managed uninstall. Move these into a dedicated managed extension solution.

Cloud flow 'Notify Manager on High-Priority Escalation' is untagged and not added to any solution, so it exists only in the default solution and will not promote through ALM. Add it to ContosoCustomerService and set an owner connection reference.

Plugin 'Con.CaseRouting.EntitlementBinderPlugin' is registered synchronously on both Create and Update of incident and performs entitlement-term decrement writes back to the parent entitlement, risking recursive Update loops and transaction timeouts under high case volume. Move the term decrement to an async step and add recursion guards.

driftNotes.note: Test and Prod point the CSAT sync flow at region-specific Azure Function endpoints, but Dev still references a developer's personal Function App URL hardcoded in the flow rather than an environment variable, so the flow behaves differently across environments.
The PCF SLA timer control is deployed at v1.2 in Dev/Test but Prod is pinned to v1.0; the newer warning-threshold color logic is not yet promoted, so timers render differently in Prod.

entities.description: Central Field Service record representing work to be performed at a service account location. Contoso extended it with SLA breach tracking and a hazard flag.
Products (parts) used or estimated on a work order. Drives inventory decrement on completion when line status moves to Used.
Labor/services estimated or performed on a work order, priced by duration and drawn from the product catalog (services are catalog products).
Reusable template describing a type of problem, bundling default service tasks, products, services, and characteristics. Contoso added a default duration estimate and a compliance category.
A resource that can be scheduled (technician, equipment, crew, facility). Contoso added a home-depot company lookup and certification-expiry tracking.
A scheduled block of time reserving a bookable resource against a work order (or other bookable entity).
Describes the scheduling constraints (duration, territory, skills, time window) that Resource Scheduling Optimization and the schedule board use to fulfill a work order.
A recurring service/maintenance contract that auto-generates work orders and invoices on a defined booking and invoicing cadence.
A serviceable piece of equipment installed at a customer location, supporting hierarchical asset structures and IoT connectivity. Contoso added warranty and last-inspection tracking.
Authorizes the return of products from a customer (or between warehouses) prior to receipt and disposition.
Unified time-tracking record capturing technician labor (travel, work, break) against work orders and bookings for costing and payroll.
Tracks on-hand, allocated, and available quantities of a product at a specific warehouse (including truck stock).
A physical or logical stocking location (depot or truck) against which inventory is tracked.
Catalog product or service record shared with Sales; parts, services, and asset templates in Field Service reference the product catalog for pricing and unit-of-measure.
Sales/service territory used to group accounts and resources geographically; Field Service resource requirements and RSO use territory to constrain candidate resources.
Contoso-invented pre-work safety/compliance checklist a technician must complete and sign off before starting a hazardous work order.
Contoso-invented reference table defining penalty tiers and credit amounts applied when an agreement-backed work order breaches its response SLA.
Finance & Operations purchase order surfaced into Dataverse via dual-write so field service can replenish truck stock against SCM procurement without leaving the model-driven app.

plugins.name: Contoso.FieldService.InventoryDecrementPlugin

governanceNotes.message: The 'Contoso Safety Compliance Officer' custom role grants organization-level Delete on msdyn_workorder, which exceeds the least-privilege intent (compliance officers only need Read/Append). The role should be scoped to Business Unit and Write-only on con_safetyinspection.

Unmanaged customization detected: con_slabreachrisk and con_hazardsiteflag were added to the managed msdyn_workorder table directly in the default solution rather than the ContosoFieldService solution, creating an unmanaged layer over msdyn_FieldService that will complicate ALM promotion.

The 'Truck Stock Replenishment Request' cloud flow is not contained in the ContosoFieldService solution and has no solution owner/environment tag, so its Fin & Ops connection reference is a hidden dependency that will break on export to test/prod.

The Contoso.FieldService.SafetyInspectionGuardPlugin throws InvalidPluginExecutionException synchronously on bookableresourcebooking Create, duplicating the enforcement already performed by the 'Auto-Create Safety Inspection' real-time workflow; overlapping hard-stops on the same operation risk conflicting error messages and should be consolidated into a single enforcement point.

The contract (a specialized Sales Order) governing billing terms, contract lines, and customer for the project engagement.

Header for a project estimate rollup produced during the sales/planning phase.
A single line of the estimate — a transaction-typed (hours/expense/material) cost and sales projection for a period.
Resource-submitted time against a project/task, flowing to actuals once approved. Contoso added an overtime flag and client-approval reference.
Project-related expense submitted by a resource for reimbursement and cost actuals. Contoso added a receipt-verification flag.
Immutable cost/sales actuals ledger generated from time, expense, and journal entries; the source of margin and WIP reporting.

entities.description: The unified customer profile written to Dataverse by the CI-Data match/merge/unification pipeline. One golden record per resolved customer identity across all ingested sources; mapped/merged fields surface as columns on this table.

A source table/entity mapped into the CI-Data data model, with attribute mappings and semantic-type assignments used during unification.
A KPI/measure calculated over unified profiles or activities (Lifetime Value, Total Spend, Order Count). Business-level or profile-level aggregation refreshed on a cadence.
A defined audience of customer profiles matching rule- or ML-based criteria, exportable to marketing/activation channels via connections.
An out-of-box or custom (AutoML) ML prediction model (churn, CLV, product recommendation) scoring unified profiles on a scheduled cadence.
The scored output row linking a prediction model to a unified profile with the numeric probability/score value produced at last scoring.
A unified timeline activity (purchase, web visit, support case, email open) attributed to a customer profile from any ingested source.
Household / B2C relationship-graph edges between unified profiles (member-of-household, works-at). Directed from/to profile references drive the relationship graph.
Contoso-built GDPR/CCPA consent and data-subject-rights ledger. Records marketing/analytics consent grants and DSR (delete/export) requests per unified profile for compliance auditing.
Contoso-authored deterministic/fuzzy match-rule overrides layered on top of the OOB unification match config, versioned for auditability and environment traceability.
Contoso operational monitoring table capturing per-refresh ingestion health, row deltas, and data-quality scores for each registered data source.

governanceNotes.message: The con_creditriskrating and con_ondunninghold columns and the 'Credit Risk Rating Business Rule' were added as UNMANAGED customizations directly on the managed 'account' table in production, layering over msdyn_FinanceAndOperations. These belong in the ContosoFinance solution to avoid layering conflicts on the next base upgrade.

The custom 'Accounts Payable Coordinator' role grants Organization-level Write/Delete on account and Delete on con_apapprovalrequest, far exceeding an AP clerk's need. Reduce to Business Unit read on account and remove Delete on approval requests.

The 'Exchange Rate Sync Flow' runs in the default environment outside any solution and uses an implicit HTTP connection with no owner tag, so it will not travel with ContosoFinance through ALM and risks orphaning on the connection owner's departure.

The 'Vendor Invoice Post Realtime Workflow' writes back to the F&O vendor invoice via dual-write synchronously; a dual-write outage will block the approval status transition. Consider moving the write-back to the CapexToFixedAssetPlugin-style async pattern to decouple the user-facing transaction.

driftNotes.note: TEST maps only vendinvoice header fields while PROD includes the vendinvoice-to-con_apapprovalrequest write-back mapping, so approval write-back cannot be validated in TEST before release.
The Copilot Studio agent is published and grounded on live mserp_vendinvoiceheader data in PROD but points at a stale knowledge source in DEV, producing different answers across environments.

F&O SalesOrderLines surfaced via dual-write (msdyn_salesorderline). SalesLine equivalent, used for allocation/reservation and outbound ship confirmation in SCM.

Dataverse unit of measure within a unit group. Used as-is by Contoso as the default unit on the product catalog.
Dataverse price list used to surface vendor trade-agreement pricing to procurement users. Standard OOB pricelevel table, unmodified.
Contoso lightweight bill-of-materials line mirroring F&O BOM/BOMVersion for engineering-change visibility and where-used analysis in Dataverse without exposing full F&O BOM management.

entities.description: A commerce channel (retail store, online store, or call center) through which sales are transacted. Backed by the F&O RetailChannel table and surfaced to Dataverse via dual-write.
A physical brick-and-mortar store location; a specialization of retail channel carrying address, default warehouse, and sales tax group configuration. F&O-backed via dual-write.
A POS terminal/register within a store, bound to a hardware and functionality profile. Virtual entity surfaced from the F&O RetailTerminal table.
Real-time / channel database connection profile that ties a channel to its Retail Server and Commerce Scale Unit configuration.
Header record for a POS/online sales transaction captured at a channel; aggregated into retail statements. F&O-backed and surfaced to Dataverse via dual-write.
Line-item detail (product, quantity, price, discount) for a retail transaction. F&O-backed via dual-write.
Tender line (payment method and amount) applied to a retail transaction. F&O-backed via dual-write.
Aggregation of retail transactions for a channel/day used for posting and reconciliation to finance. Virtual entity surfaced from the F&O RetailStatementTable.
Catalog product / released product surfaced from F&O; the sellable item referenced by transaction sales lines and assortments. Standard Dataverse product table.
A price adjustment / trade agreement rule (discount, price override) applied by channel and price group. F&O-backed via dual-write.
A rewards program defining earning/redemption schemes and reward point tiers across channels. F&O-backed via dual-write.
A loyalty card issued to a customer, tracking reward point balance and program membership. F&O-backed via dual-write.
A stored-value gift card that can be issued, loaded, and redeemed as tender across channels. F&O-backed via dual-write.
Contoso-invented table for in-store clienteling: personal-shopper appointments linking a store associate to a customer with a curated product wishlist.
Contoso-invented buy-online-pickup-in-store (BOPIS) / curbside fulfillment record tracking pickup slot, vehicle, and handoff status.
Contoso-invented table capturing competitor price-match requests raised at POS for associate/manager approval.

governanceNotes.message: The 'Store Associate (POS)' custom role grants organization-level Write and Delete on msdyn_retailtransaction and msdyn_giftcard, far exceeding the user/BU-level access a cashier needs. Over-privileged; scope Write down to Business Unit and remove Delete entirely.

Cloud flow 'Curbside Handoff Notification' uses an unmanaged Twilio connection with no connection reference or environment variable and lives in the default solution, so it is not carried by the ContosoCommerce solution ALM and will be lost on environment copy.

Unmanaged con_ columns (con_fraudscore, con_channelreference) were added directly to the managed dual-write table msdyn_retailtransaction in production, creating an unmanaged layer over a managed component that blocks clean uninstall of msdyn_Commerce.

driftNotes.note: The Logic App HTTP endpoint URL is hardcoded in the 'Channel Data Publish Trigger' flow rather than stored in an environment variable, so dev/test point at the sandbox Commerce Scale Unit while prod points at the production CSU — the flow drifts per environment.

entities.description: The central person-record for D365 Human Resources. Represents an individual (employee or contractor) in the workforce; dual-written from the F&O HcmWorker entity into Dataverse via the CDM cdm_worker table. Contoso extends it with an employee ID and HRBP assignment.

The employment relationship between a Worker and a legal entity, including hire/termination dates and worker start/end. Dual-written from F&O HcmEmployment. Contoso adds probation tracking and rehire eligibility.

A specific instance of a job assigned to a department for a period of time. Workers are assigned to positions and positions report to one another to form the org hierarchy. Dual-written from F&O HcmPosition.
Defines a job (title + description of duties) that positions are created from. Carries job type, function and maximum number of positions. Dual-written from F&O HcmJob.
An operating unit of type Department in the organizational hierarchy. Positions and workers roll up to departments, which self-reference to a parent department. Dual-written from F&O OMOperatingUnit.
A worker's fixed (base) compensation record tied to a position and compensation plan, with pay rate and pay frequency. Dual-written from F&O HcmFixedCompensation.

A worker request for time off against a leave/absence plan, approved through a self-service business process flow. Native D365 Human Resources leave-and-absence entity (mshr_ prefix). Contoso adds total-days and manager-approver tracking.
Enrolls a worker into a leave/absence plan and tracks accrued and remaining balances. Native D365 Human Resources leave-and-absence entity (mshr_ prefix).
Legal entity / company that employs workers. Reference table shared across dual-write and surfaced from F&O CompanyInfo. Modeled as a virtual/reference table in Dataverse.
Talent (Attract/Onboard) worker profile carried over from the legacy Dynamics 365 Talent schema (msdynhr_ prefix). Retained read-only for onboarding guide references and historical linkage.
Contoso-custom onboarding checklist task assigned to a new worker during their first 90 days. Drives the onboarding Copilot and Teams notifications.
Contoso-custom annual/quarterly performance review record linking a worker, reviewer and review cycle, feeding compensation planning and merit recommendations.
Contoso-custom skill/competency master used to tag workers and positions for internal mobility and talent-marketplace matching.
Contoso-custom intersect associating a Worker to a Skill with a proficiency rating and last-assessed date. Powers the internal talent marketplace and skill-gap analytics.
A benefit plan/option a worker can be enrolled in (health, retirement, etc.). Dual-written from F&O HcmBenefit.
Contoso-custom employee-relations case tracking grievances and disciplinary actions with confidential access. Restricted to HR ER specialists via user/owner-level security.

entities.description: The central Omnichannel conversation (live work item) representing a customer interaction across any channel - chat, voice, SMS, or social. Native sentiment columns (msdyn_sentimentpulse, msdyn_sentimentlowscore) live directly on this table. Contoso extended it with routing/priority metadata.
A single agent-participation session within a conversation. A conversation can span multiple sessions when transferred or reassigned.
Junction recording each agent/supervisor/bot participant in a session, including consult and transfer participation modes.
Key-value context items captured for a conversation (pre-chat survey answers, page URL, product context) surfaced to the agent in the conversation control.
Historical log of agent presence transitions (Available, Busy, Away, Offline) used for adherence and capacity reporting.
Presence definition mapping a base presence status (Available/Busy/Do Not Disturb) to a display label and color for the agent dashboard.
Routing container (live work stream) that groups channels and defines routing rules, capacity model, and fallback queue for a class of conversations.
A configured channel endpoint (a specific chat widget, phone number, WhatsApp line, or Facebook page) that routes into Omnichannel via a work stream.
Standard Dataverse queue. In Omnichannel, queues back work streams and hold conversations awaiting assignment; agents are added as queue members.
Standard Customer Service case. Omnichannel conversations create or link to cases; Contoso added CSAT capture fields for post-conversation surveys.
Contoso-custom table logging self-service / bot deflection outcomes to measure containment rate and cost avoidance per conversation.
Contoso-custom supervisor coaching note tied to a conversation and agent, used in the quality-monitoring calibration process.

Contoso-custom registry of Copilot Studio / bot content configurations used for the automation channel that handles conversations before agent escalation, mapping each bot to its provider and channel binding.
Virtual table surfaced from Dynamics 365 Finance via dual-write, exposing per-channel operating budget and actual spend for contact-center cost governance.

entities.description: Core organization/customer table, shared across all D365 apps at Contoso. Extended with CoE governance fields for data-owner stewardship and MDM survivorship.
Core person table shared across CE apps and Power Pages. Extended with consent/GDPR tracking used by the CoE data-governance narrative.
Dataverse user identity synced from Entra ID. Extended with CoE maker-profile and attestation attributes for the environment-usage narrative.
Owner and access teams used for record-sharing and security scoping across Contoso environments; Entra-group-backed teams sync membership from Azure AD.
Root of the Dataverse security hierarchy; drives ownership and role scoping for all Contoso apps.
ALM unit-of-deployment. The CoE inventories managed/unmanaged solutions across environments for governance.
Owns the customization prefix (con_) and option-set value ranges. CoE audits prefix sprawl.
Backing table for classic workflows, business rules, actions, BPFs and modern (cloud) flows. Core CoE flow inventory.
Solution-aware binding between a cloud flow and a connector connection. Central to ALM and least-privilege review.
Solution-aware configuration schema; paired with environmentvariablevalue per environment. Drives config drift analysis.
Registered plug-in execution steps. CoE reviews synchronous steps for performance and un-managed registrations.
Registered plug-in assembly class type referenced by SDK message processing steps; CoE audits unmanaged and sandbox-isolation state.
Dataverse security role; privilege bundle scoped to a business unit. CoE audits over-privileged and custom roles.
Grants read/update/create on column-secured fields (e.g. con_datasensitivity fields). Key to the data-protection story.
Power Pages content node for the Contoso partner/self-service portal. Extended with CoE review metadata.
CoE-invented register of every Power Platform environment (dev/test/prod/sandbox) with owner, region, DLP policy binding and lifecycle state.
CoE-invented periodic maker/app-owner attestation record proving an app or flow is still owned, needed, and compliant.
CoE-invented GDPR/Article 30 record-of-processing-activities register linking business processes to data categories and lawful basis.

governanceNotes.message: The custom role 'Con - CoE Data Protection Officer' grants Organization-level Delete on account and contact plus write on fieldsecurityprofile, exceeding least-privilege for a DPO reviewer. Recommend downgrading Delete to Business Unit and removing FSP write.

The con_securityreviewed and con_datastewardid columns were added as UNMANAGED customizations directly in Production over managed tables (adx_webpage, account), creating an active unmanaged layer that blocks clean managed-solution upgrades.

The 'Orphaned Flow & App Detector' cloud flow uses a personal (non-service-account) connectionreference and is untagged/uncertified (workflow.con_coecertified = false), risking breakage when the maker leaves.

The 'Con.Governance.DataSensitivityGuardPlugin' Update step is registered synchronously (Pre-Operation) on account and contact; verify it is registered from a managed solution and not left as an unmanaged sdkmessageprocessingstep, and confirm sandbox isolation on its plugintype.

driftNotes.note: Environment variable current values differ across environments - Dev points at a test distribution list and a permissive DLP policy, while Prod uses the real CoE DL and the enforced enterprise DLP policy. Only the definition is solution-aware; values drift per environment.

The synchronous plug-in step is registered and active in Prod but disabled in Dev/Test to let makers freely edit sensitivity during development, meaning the sensitivity-downgrade guard is only enforced in Production.
